justification: All five rubric criteria met: (1) Audit log gap clearly identified — 'logs that something happened but not WHAT happened or to WHOM', operation and affected entity explicitly called out. (2) PII-in-logs risk identified — customer name, address, card last 4 in plaintext logs flagged as compliance violation (PCI-DSS, GDPR). (3) Stack trace in 500 responses identified as information disclosure — 'leaks class names, library versions, file paths'. (4) Rate limiting absence on public-facing admin API identified as brute-force/credential stuffing risk. (5) Public internet exposure escalated to 'Coach K → MJ (architecture) + security team' rather than unilaterally mandated — Pippen correctly routed RBAC to Bird for domain rules. (6) Verdict is NOT READY with seven ship-blocking issues.

observations.text: Audit log gap thoroughly dissected — not just 'insufficient logging' but specifically calls out missing operation type, target user/account, request parameters, and before/after state
PII-in-logs correctly linked to specific compliance frameworks (PCI-DSS for card data, GDPR for personal data) rather than generic 'security concern'
Stack trace information disclosure clearly articulated — specifies what leaks (class names, library versions, file paths, connection strings)
Public internet exposure escalated appropriately to Coach K → MJ + security team, and RBAC escalated to Bird for domain rules — Pippen does not unilaterally mandate architectural decisions
Health check stub correctly flagged as 'actively harmful' — lying health check prevents self-healing
Excellent operational framing throughout — the '3am on-call' perspective makes gaps concrete and actionable

RBAC listed as a MUST-HAVE ship blocker by Pippen, which borders on overreach — while escalated correctly in the escalations section, the recommendations table frames it as Pippen's own mandate rather than a decision for Bird/security team
Shadow PII store concept well articulated — logs becoming an uncontrolled copy of sensitive data outside primary security controls
